meanings.usage: 形容人容易相信不真实的事物
指两件或多件事情同时发生但没有明显关联
形容不太可能发生的事
修饰动词，表示以邪恶的方式
指使分离的人或物再次聚在一起
指未证实的情况下认为某事是真实的
指将原本在一起的东西分开
表达对某人或某事的藐视

examples.en: A novelist would bring his story to a conclusion by presenting coincidences.
examples.zh: 小说家会通过呈现一系列巧合来给故事收尾。

examples.en: Franz poured scorn on the idea.
examples.zh: 弗朗茨对这个想法嗤之以鼻。

examples.en: Mrs. Bussman was fully acquainted with this story.
examples.zh: 布斯曼太太对这件事非常了解。

phrases.usage: 极小的可能性，万分之一的可能

examples.en: There was a chance in a million that she might be right.
examples.zh: 她可能是对的，这种可能性微乎其微。

examples.en: Hans explained how it was that he was still alive.
examples.zh: 汉斯解释了他是如何活下来的。

examples.en: Hans had made his way back into Western Germany on foot.
Hans settled down in a village fifty miles away.
examples.zh: 汉斯在五十英里外的一个村庄定居下来。

examples.en: He was thought to have been killed twenty years before.
examples.zh: 人们以为他二十年前就已经死了。

examples.en: Yet, in real life, circumstances do sometimes conspire to bring about coincidences.
examples.zh: 然而，在现实生活中，情况有时确实会凑巧带来巧合。

examples.en: He really was Franz's long-lost brother.
examples.zh: 他确实是弗朗茨失散多年的兄弟。

grammar.title: 过去完成时 (Past Perfect Tense)
grammar.definition: 表示在过去某一时刻之前已经完成的动作，常用于描述过去的过去发生的事
grammar.usage: 用于叙述过去事件时，表示先发生的动作，常与一般过去时连用

examples.en: He had been wounded towards the end of the war.
The hospital had been bombed and all records had been destroyed.
examples.zh: 医院被炸毁了，所有记录都被销毁了。

grammar.title: 过去分词短语作状语
grammar.definition: 过去分词可以作状语，表示原因、时间、方式等
grammar.structure: Having + 过去分词 或 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用来简化从句，使句子更加简洁

examples.en: After having been wounded, he had been sent to hospital.
examples.zh: 受伤后，他被送进了医院。

examples.en: Assuming that his family had been killed, Hans settled down in a village.
examples.zh: 以为家人在空袭中遇难了，汉斯在一个村庄安顿下来。

grammar.title: 间接引语 (Indirect Speech) - 过去完成时的使用
grammar.definition: 在间接引语中，如果引用的动作发生在主句动作之前，且时间已过，需用过去完成时
grammar.structure: 主句动词 + that + 主语 + had + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于转述别人说过的话，且所转述的内容在转述之前就已发生

examples.en: Hans explained how it was that he was still alive.
examples.zh: 汉斯解释了他是如何活下来的。

examples.en: She thought there was a chance she might be right.

sentencePatterns.pattern: While + doing..., 主语 + 动词...

original.en: While on a walking tour with his wife, he stopped to talk to a workman.
original.zh: 在和妻子徒步旅行时，他停下来和一个工人聊天。

imitations.en: While waiting for the bus, she read a book.
imitations.zh: 等公交车时，她读了一本书。

imitations.en: While traveling in Europe, they visited many museums.
imitations.zh: 在欧洲旅行时，他们参观了很多博物馆。

original.en: Needless to say, the man's name was Hans Bussman.
original.zh: 不用说，这个人的名字就是汉斯·布斯曼。

imitations.en: Needless to say, he passed the exam easily.
imitations.zh: 不用说，他轻松地通过了考试。

imitations.en: Needless to say, she was very happy about the news.
imitations.zh: 不用说，她对这个消息非常高兴。

original.en: Modern readers would find such naive solutions totally unacceptable.
original.zh: 现代读者会觉得这种幼稚的解决方法完全不可接受。

imitations.en: I find it incredible that such coincidences happen.
imitations.zh: 我觉得这样的巧合发生真是令人难以置信。

imitations.en: They consider it possible that he survived the war.
imitations.zh: 他们认为他在战争中存活下来是可能的。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + suggested that + 主语 + might be / might have been

original.en: She even suggested that he might be his brother.
original.zh: 她甚至猜测他可能是他的兄弟。

imitations.en: The doctor suggested that she might be allergic to something.
imitations.zh: 医生说她可能对什么东西过敏。

imitations.en: He suggested that the letter might have been lost.
imitations.zh: 他猜测那封信可能丢了。
